1.definition: Up to 5% chance of a tornado within 25 miles of your location.

2.definition: 5-10% chance of a tornado within 25 miles of your location.

3.definition: 10-15% chance of a tornado within 25 miles of your location.

4.definition: 15-30% or greater chance of a tornado within 25 miles of your location.

5.definition: 30% or greater chance of a tornado within 25 miles of your location.

0.definition: Little to no Severe Thunderstorm Wind Risk

1.definition: Up to 15% chance of severe wind gusts (58 mph or greater) within 25 miles of your location.

2.definition: 15-30% chance of severe wind gusts (58 mph or greater) within 25 miles of your location.

3.definition: 30-45% chance of severe wind gusts (58 mph or greater) within 25 miles of your location.

4.definition: 45-60% chance of severe wind gusts (58 mph or greater) with a 10% or greater chance of extreme wind gusts (74 mph or greater) within 25 miles of your location.

5.definition: 60% or greater chance of severe wind gusts (58 mph or greater) with a 10% or greater chance of extreme wind gusts (74 mph or greater) within 25 miles of your location.

1.definition: Up to 15% chance of 1" or larger hail within 25 miles of your location.

2.definition: 15-30% chance of 1" or larger hail within 25 miles of your location.

3.definition: 30-45% chance of 1" or larger hail within 25 miles of your location.

4.definition: 45% or greater chance of 1" or larger hail within 25 miles of your location.

1.definition: Isolated to scattered thunderstorms with dangerous cloud-to-ground lightning expected.

2.definition: Numerous thunderstorms with dangerous cloud-to-ground lightning expected.

3.definition: Widespread thunderstorms with dangerous cloud-to-ground lightning expected.

1.definition: Isolated flash floods possible. Most flooding will be localized to areas that can experience rapid runoff with heavy rainfall.

2.definition: Scattered flash floods possible, some potentially significant. Most vulnerable are urban areas, roads, small streams, and washes.

3.definition: Numerous flash floods including some significant events likely. Many streams may flood, affecting nearby residential and urban areas.

4.definition: Widespread, severe flash floods expected. Events could occur in areas that don't normally flood. Lives and property in greater danger.

1.definition: Heat exhaustion possible with prolonged exposure.

2.definition: Heat exhaustion likely with prolonged exposure. Heat stroke possible.

3.definition: Heat exhaustion or heat stroke likely with prolonged exposure.

4.definition: Dangerously hot conditions could quickly result in heat exhaustion or heat stroke.

1.definition: Breezy conditions could blow around unsecured items and result in hazardous driving conditions for high-profile vehicles.

2.definition: Windy conditions could damage outdoor items and result in hazardous driving conditions for high-profile vehicles.

3.definition: Very windy conditions could blow down trees and cause isolated power outages.

4.definition: Extremely windy conditions will damage numerous trees and cause widespread power outages.

0.definition: No danger of frost or freeze causing damage to vegetation.

1.definition: Frost could cause minor damage to unprotected vegetation.

2.definition: A light freeze could damage unprotected vegetation.

3.definition: A moderate freeze could cause significant damage to unprotected vegetation.

4.definition: A hard freeze could cause catastrophic damage to unprotected vegetation.

1.definition: Fog with visibilities less than or equal to 1 mile.

2.definition: Fog with visibilities less than or equal to 1/2 mile.

3.definition: Fog with visibilities less than or equal to 1/4 mile.

1.definition: Low humidity and breezy conditions will result in an increased danger of fires getting out of control.

2.definition: Low humidity and windy conditions will create dangerous fire conditions. Fires can escape control more easily and be difficult to contain.

3.definition: High winds and extended dry periods will produce extreme burning conditions. Open fires can quickly escape and are very difficult to control, even for experienced fire fighters.

4.definition: Very strong winds, combined with low humidity, will create extremely volatile conditions. Fires will spread very quickly with spot fires common. Fire control will be extremely difficult.

1.definition: Large breaking waves will be hazardous to anyone who enters the surf.

2.definition: Large breaking waves will produce dangerous swimming and surfing conditions and localized beach erosion.

3.definition: Life-threatening swimming conditions and significant beach erosion can be expected.

4.definition: Life-threatening swimming conditions and major beach erosion can be expected.

0.definition: The risk for rip currents is low, however, life-threatening rip currents often occur in the vicinity of groins, jetties, and piers.

2.definition: Life-threatening rip currents are possible in the surf zone.

3.definition: Life-threatening rip currents are likely in the surf zone.

1.definition: Wind or Wind Gusts >= 25 knots and/or Waves >= 5 feet.

2.definition: Wind or Wind Gusts >= 34 knots and/or Waves >= 8 feet.

3.definition: Wind or Wind Gusts >= 48 knots and/or Waves >= 10 feet.

4.definition: Wind or Wind Gusts >= 64 knots and/or Waves >= 15 feet.
